rxn_class_summary: Electrochemically driven allylation of unactivated primary/secondary/tertiary alkyl bromides with allylic carbonates to form C(sp3)–C(sp3) bonds via radical–polar crossover, without transition-metal catalysts
electrochemical_role: Cathodic single-electron reduction of alkyl bromide generates alkyl radical; a second cathodic reduction of the benzylic radical intermediate generates a carbanion that undergoes β-elimination of carbonate

key_intermediates.name: tertiary benzylic radical from addition to allylic carbonate
benzylic carbanion after second cathodic reduction
allylated alkane product after β-elimination of OCOOEt

electron_flow: Cathode reduces alkyl bromide to an alkyl radical, which adds to the allylic carbonate's alkene to give a stabilized tertiary benzylic radical; this radical is further reduced at the cathode to a carbanion that undergoes β-elimination of the ethyl carbonate to deliver the allylated product.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: DPE-trapped alkyl radical adducts 8 (36%), 9 (47%), 10 (61%) isolated, confirming alkyl radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: TEMPO–alkyl adducts 11 (21%) and 12 (18%) observed; 13 (tBu) not detected; reaction suppressed

probe_reagents.name: (bromomethyl)cyclopropane (2d)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened allylated product 14 obtained in 55%, indicating primary alkyl radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: ethyl 2-bromo-2-cyclopropylacetate (2e)
probe_reagents.observation: Desired product 15 in trace; ring-opened by-product 15a in 45%, supporting secondary alkyl radical formation

probe_reagents.name: allyl-tethered bromotetrahydrofuran substrates (2f, 2g)
probe_reagents.observation: 5-exo radical cyclization products 16 (67%) and 17 (31%) isolated, confirming alkyl radical intermediacy

probe_reagents.name: competition between 1°, 2°, 3° alkyl bromides
probe_reagents.observation: Tertiary (3a, 59%) > secondary (5a, 17%) > primary (4a, 8%), consistent with ease of C–Br reduction / radical stability

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of alkyl bromides and allylic carbonate
probe_reagents.observation: Ep/2: tBu-Br −2.52 V, iPr-Br −2.66 V, 3-PhPrBr −2.70 V; allylic carbonate 1a −2.40, −2.74 V vs SCE, showing alkyl bromides are reduced under conditions

notes: Authors explicitly ruled out cathodic reduction of the allylic carbonate to release an allyl radical (not observed). FeXn is shown at the anode in the proposed scheme, suggesting sacrificial Fe anode chemistry, though it is not emphasized mechanistically. TBAB serves as supporting electrolyte in DMA at 4 mA, r.t.
